stage_prompt.name: Cleanup — Remove Platform Tools from BlarAI

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Present structured summary. STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval.

v2_updates.note: Additive to v1 work_items 5.1–5.12. Apply alongside.

item.name: Verify v2-baseline platform files are gone after recursive deletes
item.description: v1 item 5.1 deletes 7 dirs + 2 files. v2 has additional platform-only files in those dirs that were copied to devplatform in Stage 3.1.v2. Recursive delete already covers them; just add explicit verification.

item.name: Move governance docs and wake templates out of BlarAI/docs/
item.description: v1 item 5.5 deletes platform-doctrine docs but doesn't address `docs/governance/` (7 files) or `docs/scheduled/wake_templates/` (6 templates). Copied to devplatform in Stage 3.2.v2 — now DELETE from BlarAI.

stay_in_blarai.a: docs/active_tasks.yaml — moves to .platform/active_tasks.yaml per v1 item 5.3

move_to_devplatform.a: tools/scheduled-tasks/escalation-watchdog.* — platform task (already part of the live 13-task fleet)

item.name: Test baseline expectation update
item.description: v1 item 5.8 expects 755 passed / 2 skipped. v2 baseline (per CLAUDE.md §Active State) is **~981 passed, 22 skipped** post-Sprint-8.
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1; pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q

stage_prompt.objective: Remove dormant platform tool directories and state files from BlarAI. Relocate BlarAI-side coordination artifacts (sprints, scheduled task templates, active_tasks.yaml) into `BlarAI/.platform/` so BlarAI still owns its own project state but not the platform machinery. Merge `chore/platform-extraction` to BlarAI main.

preconditions.check: Stage 4 complete for at least 24 hours.
Fleet has executed scheduled tasks from devplatform successfully at least twice.

item.name: Archive fleet audit logs before recursive deletion (S-2 fix)
item.description: **S-2 fix.** Several platform tool directories slated for recursive deletion in 5.1 contain audit-relevant log files (cadence monitor history, watchdog event logs, autonomy-budget state snapshots, escalation notifications). Recursive `Remove-Item -Force` deletes them silently. Archive everything matching `*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.json.bak`, and `state.json*` from the about-to-be-deleted directories into a single tarball under `BlarAI/.platform/audit_archive/` before any deletion. Cheap insurance — total size is typically < 50 MB; the archive is read-only forensic data.
item.command:
$archiveRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.platform\audit_archive' New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $archiveRoot -Force | Out-Null $stamp = (Get-Date).ToString('yyyyMMdd_HHmmss') $stagingDir = Join-Path $archiveRoot "pre_stage5_$stamp" New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $stagingDir -Force | Out-Null
$sourceDirs = @( 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\fleet_ops', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\fleet_observability', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\autonomy_budget', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\gate_stale_cleaner', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\scheduled-tasks', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\vikunja_mcp' ) $patterns = @('*.log', '*.jsonl', '*.json.bak', 'state.json*', '*.history') $copied = 0 foreach ($dir in $sourceDirs) { if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { continue } foreach ($pat in $patterns) { Get-ChildItem -Path $dir -Filter $pat -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $rel = $_.FullName.Substring('C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\'.Length) $dst = Join-Path $stagingDir $rel New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $dst -Parent) -Force | Out-Null Copy-Item -LiteralPath $_.FullName -Destination $dst -Force $copied++ } } } $tarball = Join-Path $archiveRoot "pre_stage5_$stamp.zip" Compress-Archive -Path "$stagingDir\*" -DestinationPath $tarball -CompressionLevel Optimal Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stagingDir Write-Host "Archived $copied audit files to $tarball" if ($copied -eq 0) { Write-Warning "No audit files matched. Verify expected logs exist before proceeding to 5.1." }
item.expected: True. Archive must exist before 5.1 runs.
item.preserve: The archive lives under `.platform/` so it survives Stage 5.1 recursive deletes (5.1 only targets `tools/` subdirs).

item.name: Delete platform tool directories from BlarAI

keep_in_blarai.item: tools/tests/ — keep BlarAI-specific tests; delete only platform tool tests.

item.name: Create BlarAI/.platform/ directory for BlarAI-side coordination state
Move BlarAI project coordination files into .platform/
item.description: These files describe BlarAI's current work (sprints, active tasks, scheduled-task templates). They are BlarAI-owned but read by the devplatform fleet. The <moves> block below is an inventory; the <command> is the executable form.
item.preserve: `.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml` from Stage 1.5 stays — it's the reverse lookup.

item.name: Update devplatform fleet tools to look in .platform/ for coordination files
item.description: Any path references to `sprints/`, `scheduled/`, `docs/active_tasks.yaml` inside devplatform fleet tools now need to resolve via ProjectContext + `.platform/` subdirectory. E.g., `project_root / "sprints"` becomes `project_root / ".platform" / "sprints"`.

item.name: Move platform-layer docs out of BlarAI docs/
item.description: Docs that are platform-doctrine (Co-Lead protocols, SDO initiation prompts, DEC proposals, DOMAIN docs, CLAUDE_* governance) should be DELETED from BlarAI/docs/ — they live in devplatform/docs/ now. BlarAI/docs/ retains only BlarAI-product docs (ADRs, Task4, P5_*, ledgers, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, TEST_GOVERNANCE.md).
item.preserve: This `docs/platform_separation/` folder stays in BlarAI — it's the migration record.

item.name: Update BlarAI pyproject.toml to remove platform-tool test paths
item.description: Edit `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` testpaths and markers — remove anything referencing tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget.

item.name: Update BlarAI .gitignore
item.description: Add `.platform/` contents handling. Typically `.platform/` SHOULD be tracked (sprints and active tasks are project state), but secrets/tokens inside should be gitignored.

item.name: Run BlarAI test suite to confirm no regressions
item.expected: Same baseline (755 passed, 2 skipped). Or full scope (835 passed, 2 skipped) if live runtime available.

item.name: Verify fleet still operates end-to-end from devplatform
Commit chore/platform-extraction — HALT for Lead Architect approval before merge to main
item.description: **Lead Architect gate.** Commit on the working branch but DO NOT merge to `main` autonomously. Per repo policy, all merges to main require explicit Lead Architect approval. Print the diff summary, the test baseline, and stop. Wait for explicit "merge approved" before executing the merge block below.
item.gate: Lead Architect must explicitly approve before the merge block is executed. Until approval, the working branch is the only carrier of Stages 2-5 changes.

item.name: Tag the post-extract state on both repos
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git tag post-platform-extract -m "BlarAI cleanup complete, devplatform authoritative"; cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git tag platform-extracted -m "devplatform established from BlarAI extraction"

item.name: Update STATUS.md
item.description: Mark Stage 5 COMPLETE. Record: both commit hashes, tags, test baseline confirmation, deletion counts.

rollback.trigger: BlarAI tests regress, or fleet task fails after cleanup.

steps.step: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git reset --hard pre-platform-extract # revert to Stage-0 tag
That restores all deleted tools, docs, sprints/, scheduled/, active_tasks.yaml in place.
MCP configs stay pointing at devplatform (Stage 4) unless also rolled back.
Decide: keep devplatform + restored BlarAI (BlarAI has redundant copies) OR full rollback to before Stage 4 via RECOVERY.md level-4 procedure.

exit_criteria.criterion: All 7 platform tool directories removed from BlarAI/tools/.
BlarAI/.platform/ contains sprints/, scheduled/, active_tasks.yaml, vikunja_project_ids.yaml.
Platform doctrine docs removed from BlarAI/docs/.
BlarAI test baseline (755 passed / 2 skipped) preserved.
Fleet scheduled task executes successfully post-cleanup.
